sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ['B', 'W', 'c', 'G']:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']),
                  sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']))
        if center[0] > 5:
            print(f"Node: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, center~({center[0]:.0f},{center[1]:.0f})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

Let me continue moving this block towards the blob to see if it triggers game-over or level completion.
Actually, I realize I should be more strategic. I've been moving one block incrementally, which takes many actions. Let me try to move the block directly to a position adjacent to the blob in one click, to quickly test if that triggers game-over or level completion.
The blob is at (27, 33) with 69 pixels. The blob's radius is roughly sqrt(69/π) ≈ 4.7 pixels. So the blob's boundary is about 5 pixels from the center.
If I move the block to (22, 33), it would be 5 pixels above the blob's center, which is right at the boundary. This might trigger absorption or game-over.
Let me try clicking at (22, 33) to move the block there.
Wait, but the block is at (30, 24) and (22, 33) is about sqrt(8^2 + 9^2) ≈ 12 pixels away. The block might not be able to move that far in one click.
Let me try clicking at (28, 28) which is closer to the block and also closer to the blob.
Actually, let me just try to move the block to a position very close to the blob, like (25, 30), and see what happens.
